_comment: Recurring price-window priors. These are PRIORS, not facts: the planner treats them as hypotheses to be confirmed by live web search and by the maturing baseline data. Edit, prune, or add freely. 'months' is the months the window falls in; 'type' is the mechanism; 'confidence' is how reliable the prior is.

patterns.note: New Year itself is a price PEAK in Antalya; the days right after collapse as resorts shed unsold holiday inventory. 5-star all-inclusive can fall sharply.
Oct-Nov 'velvet season' and Jan-Mar (excluding NYE) are the cheapest. A cheap 5-star here in winter is partly just low season - the deal is one that's cheap even for the season.

patterns.city: Bulgarian Black Sea coast (Burgas, Nessebar, Sozopol)
patterns.window: shoulder season late May & early-mid September
patterns.note: Still warm, but a fraction of Jul-Aug peak prices. Marti's classic window - 5-star all-inclusive has gone for ~50 EUR/night for 3.

patterns.city: Bulgarian Black Sea coast (Burgas, Nessebar, Sozopol)
patterns.note: Very cheap but many resorts closed; the find is an open luxury property at off-season prices.
Ski peak is Jan-Mar. Outside ski + summer-hike season, luxury chalets/spas drop hard.
Milanese leave for holidays; business hotels empty out and discount heavily.

patterns.window: the week AFTER Fashion Week / Salone del Mobile
patterns.note: Fashion Weeks (Feb/Sep womenswear, Jan/Jun menswear) and Salone del Mobile (mid-April design week) spike prices; the days right after see a sharp drop. Needs live search to pin exact dates each year.
Hot, emptier August and quiet Jan-Feb. (2025 Jubilee inflated prices; 2026 should normalize - confirm via search.)
Christmas-market and NYE peak ends; deep quiet through Feb.
Cheapest outside spring/autumn shoulders and summer. Watch for the post-NYE drop too.
Deepest hotel discounts of the year right after Christmas.
NOT in your city list, but worth knowing: Parisians leave in August and many hotels discount. The week after September Fashion Week also drops. (2024 Olympics was a one-off exception.)
Not in your list; extreme summer heat empties luxury hotels - 5-star prices can fall 50-70%.
Not in your list; acqua-alta season and deep winter are cheapest.

shock_types_to_watch: major event ending (festival, conference, trade fair, sporting final) - prices drop sharply the following days
terror attack, political unrest, or safety scare - tourism dries up temporarily and prices collapse for the brave
natural disruption (heatwave, flooding aftermath, ash cloud) suppressing demand
new low-cost airline route or base announced - sudden cheap access can precede hotel-side adjustment
currency weakness making a destination unusually cheap for EUR holders (e.g. Turkish lira slides)
large hotel or resort newly opened / re-opened running aggressive launch pricing
a destination falling out of fashion or a bad-press cycle softening demand
